test.command: cd apps/ios && xcodebuild -project ADE.xcodeproj -scheme ADE -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,id=2107A402-C2A7-4323-AF26-74A0AC406C44' -derivedDataPath /tmp/ade-build test

typecheck.command: cd apps/ios && xcodebuild -project ADE.xcodeproj -scheme ADE -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,id=2107A402-C2A7-4323-AF26-74A0AC406C44' -derivedDataPath /tmp/ade-build build

lint.command: cd apps/ios && xcodebuild -project ADE.xcodeproj -scheme ADE -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,id=2107A402-C2A7-4323-AF26-74A0AC406C44' -derivedDataPath /tmp/ade-build analyze

blockingIssues.description: Initial hydration is launched in an untracked task, so a stale 15-second wait from an older connection can survive disconnect/reconnect and later overwrite a newer healthy connection's domain statuses.
sendEnvelope handles any send callback error as active, so a late cancellation error from an intentionally closed socket can tear down a newly connected socket during reconnect or host switching.

SyncUserFacingError still falls back to raw technical strings like `Invalid hello response.` and `Unknown queued operation type.`, so the feature does not fully meet the user-friendly sync error requirement.
VAL-DS-007 remains unmet because Files swipe actions do not expose delete/rename and Work swipe actions do not expose archive/pin, even though the feature claims that assertion.

appliedUpdates.description: Removed `xcodebuild ... | tail` pipelines from the iOS build/test commands and added explicit `typecheck` and `lint` commands so scrutiny validators preserve real exit codes.
Documented in `.factory/library/architecture.md` that long-lived `SyncService` tasks and WebSocket callbacks must be scoped to the active connection and canceled or ignored on disconnect/host switch.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update `.factory/skills/ios-worker/SKILL.md` to use raw `xcodebuild` commands (or `set -o pipefail`) instead of `| tail` pipelines in its validation procedure and examples.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The sync-reliability-and-ux review found the skill still recommends tailed `xcodebuild` commands, and that worker explicitly had to deviate because the pipeline can hide failing exit codes.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update the generated iOS mission AGENTS template to use direct `xcodebuild` commands, or require `pipefail` when truncating output for display.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The sync-reliability-and-ux review found this mission's AGENTS instructions still used tailed build/test commands, which conflicts with trustworthy validator execution.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Resolve the ios-worker skill's simulator-verification contradiction by removing simulator-based `interactiveChecks` from the example handoff or changing the procedure if simulator interaction is actually expected.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The design-system-overhaul review found the skill tells workers not to launch the simulator or take screenshots, but its example handoff still includes simulator-based interactive checks.

rejectedObservations.observation: A worker launched the simulator and captured screenshots even though the mission AGENTS file and ios-worker skill already forbid that for iOS validation.
rejectedObservations.reason: already-documented; existing instructions are explicit, so this is a worker compliance/handoff accuracy problem rather than a missing shared-state update
